title: Dell VxRail Physical Disk Predictive Failure and Unhealthy Drive Status
dataSources: `index=vxrail` `sourcetype="vxrail:physical_disk"` from VxRail Manager REST (`/v2/system/disks` family) or `sourcetype="idrac:sel"` with `predictive_failure`, `slot`, `serial_number`
app: Custom (VxRail Manager REST API), optional `Splunk Add-on for Dell EMC` or iDRAC syslog
spl:
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:physical_disk" earliest=-24h
| where predictive_failure=1 OR lower(disk_state) IN ("failed", "offline", "unknown") OR smart_alert=1
| stats values(disk_state) as state, values(predictive_failure) as pred by host, serial_number, slot
| sort host, slot
description: VxRail nodes depend on local drives for cache and capacity tiers before vSAN rebuilds complete. Predictive failure and offline disk states are leading indicators of rebuild traffic, reduced fault tolerance, and potential double-fault risk.
value: Lets operators open Dell support cases and schedule drive swaps during business hours instead of during a vSAN resync emergency.
implementation: Extend your existing VxRail modular input to poll physical disk inventory per node. Map vendor fields to `predictive_failure`, `smart_alert`, and `disk_state`. Supplement with iDRAC SEL forwarded as syslog for cross-check. Alert on first predictive flag per serial.
visualization: Table (at-risk disks by node), Single value (predictive count), Timeline (disk state changes).
